meta.generator: GPT-5.5 draft mapping from pci-emails-corpus.json and dp-context-for-pci-mapping.json
notes: AI-generated mapping reviewed by Opus. Requires steward sign-off before rendering on DP pages.
Relationship=informed means the email directly contributes a concept or requirement to the DP.
Relationship=aligned means thematic support or resonance with an existing DP.
Relationship=context means useful historical background but weaker direct provenance.

mappings.title: Meta-Layer Initiative Meeting with Vint Cerf
mappings.author: Anon

dp_links.summary: Frames the Meta-Layer as protocol-agnostic infrastructure for overlay applications and smart tags across webpages.
Introduces portable communities that travel through the web experience.
Names data sovereignty and privacy as baseline requirements for the environment.
Calls for safely enabling and using AI in the application substrate.

mappings.title: Anticipating PCEs and Meta-Layer Alignment
mappings.author: Cindy Mason

dp_links.summary: Discusses personal computing environments, private clouds, autonomy, privacy, and control over personal and biodata.
Describes PCEs as personal guardrails shielding users from harms in AI-driven systems.
Warns about GenAI and dataset provenance issues that affect safe and ethical AI design.

mappings.title: Meta-Layer Initiative for Decentralized and Transparent Web
mappings.author: Anon

dp_links.summary: Requires networks of real people in good standing while allowing pseudo-anonymity.
Calls for decentralized structures supported by AI-assisted community moderation.
Emphasizes seamless interaction across all digital spaces rather than isolated silos.
Centers persistent tools for meeting, interacting, collaborating, and annotating across the web.
Names control over one’s own data as a requirement for thriving human networks.

mappings.title: Metalevel Initiative: Challenges and Opportunities
mappings.author: Paul Werbos

mappings.title: AI-Enhanced Collective Intelligence for Global Challenges
mappings.author: David Bray, PhD

dp_links.summary: Argues that collective intelligence paired with AI agents can help large/global networks coordinate.
Centers AI agents that listen to humans rather than manipulate attention.
Relates to feedback mechanisms for emergent collaboration and crowd judgment.

mappings.title: The Overweb: Building a Decentralized Meta-Layer
mappings.author: Anon

dp_links.summary: Defines the Overweb as a safe decentralized environment where communities meet, interact, and collaborate above webpages.
States that individuals own their data and participate in a privacy-respecting environment.
Explains a layer above webpages using current protocols, browser technology, cryptography, and AI without needing a major breakthrough.
Mentions fair value exchange for participant contributions.
Rejects centralized platform control and points toward shared infrastructure outside government or Big Tech ownership.

mappings.title: Augmented Conversations and AI-Assisted Collaboration in the Overweb
mappings.author: Anon

dp_links.summary: Connects augmented conversations, shared simulations, and global collaboration to the Meta-Layer vision.
Positions AI as a participant in collaborative work that must enhance human capability without exploiting attention.
Requires trustworthy shared truth: what one participant sees, all participants see, with auditable effects.
Describes a shared information ecology connecting content snippets through explicit relationships.
Emphasizes interoperability between connected information, simulations, and collaborative tools.

mappings.title: Defining Goals for Effective Collective Intelligence
mappings.author: David Bray, PhD

dp_links.summary: Defines collective intelligence as requiring divergent perspectives around shared goals.
Highlights the need to define outcomes and goals upfront for networks of teams or society.

mappings.title: Innovation Studios: Designing for Collective Intelligence in the Overweb
mappings.author: Anon

dp_links.summary: Proposes innovation studios as collaborative spaces for applying collective intelligence to Overweb systems.
Describes synthesis, moderation, and feedback processes for proposals emerging from multiple groups.
Links value creation and verification to compensation and contributor rewards.
Provides a process model for decentralized community design and governance.
Points to shared information ecosystems and community participation in substrate design.

mappings.title: Future of Interaction: Moving Beyond Webpages
mappings.author: David Bray, PhD

dp_links.summary: Challenges the webpage-centric model and calls for support across video, audio, voice, text, and body language.
Suggests interoperability among personal AI agents without centralized hosting platforms.

mappings.title: Acknowledging Contributions in Collaborative Spaces
mappings.author: Cindy Mason

dp_links.summary: Stresses acknowledging contributors so their input remains visible and career-sustaining.
Relates contribution recognition to reputation and feedback mechanisms.
Points to the economic need for contributors to feed families and be compensated.

mappings.title: Collective Intelligence and Brain Synchronization
mappings.author: Paul Werbos

dp_links.summary: Develops collective intelligence as a fundamental challenge requiring many communities and ideas.

mappings.title: Cross-Cultural and Multilingual AI Tools
mappings.author: David Michaelis

mappings.title: Digital Cognitive Pathways and Metaweb Evolution
mappings.author: Anon

dp_links.summary: Introduces Digital Cognitive Pathways across webpages, VR, AR, audio, video, and AI-driven interfaces.
Proposes hashing terrains and artifacts so annotations remain consistent and traceable across mediums.
Requires AI-agent interactions to be logged on-chain in human-readable form for trust and accountability.
Supports interoperability across terrains, artifacts, agents, and annotation layers.

mappings.title: Recognizing and Rewarding Contributions in the Metaweb
mappings.author: Anon

dp_links.summary: Explores fiat or token-based rewards tied directly to valuable contributions.
Frames fair compensation for connections, verification, and innovation-studio contributions.
Calls for contribution tracking and reputation-like attribution mechanisms.
Emphasizes transparent decentralized attribution for valuable insights.

mappings.title: Call for Ideas: Help Shape the Future of the Web
mappings.author: Anon

dp_links.summary: Invites the PCI community into the Call for Ideas and movement-building around the Meta-Layer.
Mentions digital badges recognizing contributors to the call for input.

mappings.title: AI and Humanity's Deepest Problems: Love and Compassion
mappings.author: Cindy Mason

dp_links.summary: Asks whether AI can help with compassion, love, forgiveness, and human flourishing.

mappings.title: Fostering Serendipitous Digital Connections through the Meta-Layer
mappings.author: Anon

dp_links.summary: Presents the meta-layer as a safe space for meaningful serendipitous connection and collaboration above webpages.
Requires authentic, accountable interactions to avoid scammers and predators.
Emphasizes participant purpose, belonging, and self-directed connection.
Connects safe relational computing to privacy and trust.

mappings.title: Navigating Existential Threats with AGI and Meta-Frameworks
mappings.author: Paul Werbos

dp_links.summary: Argues that the metaframework must address AGI-level minefield decision problems and information-flow restrictions.
Discusses AGI, conflict-of-interest risks, and the need for AI systems aligned with human survival.
Frames governance and decision design as essential to avoid systemic instability.
Calls for minefield-aware strategy and future-risk planning.
Raises cybersecurity and sensitive information-flow restrictions as survival issues.

mappings.title: Human-Centered AI: A Critical Perspective
mappings.author: Cindy Mason

dp_links.summary: Critiques “human-centered AI” and argues caring AI must protect people rather than exploit them.
Focuses on data duty of care, data economy extraction, surveillance, and protecting personal data.
Warns that AI language can obscure exploitative behavior unless transparency is required.

mappings.title: Legal Liabilities in the Meta-Layer
mappings.author: Brian Donohue

dp_links.summary: Warns that governance must account for legal rights, liabilities, and human responsibility.
Links trust to transparent understanding of legal accountability in machine-mediated systems.

mappings.title: Strategic Opportunity for Internet Reform with New Leadership
mappings.author: Anon

dp_links.summary: Proposes decentralized, community-based governance as part of internet reform.
Centers privacy, data sovereignty, and digital rights against surveillance and platform control.
Frames decentralized information infrastructure as community-controlled digital commons.
Calls for trust, transparency, and information integrity in decentralized systems.
Supports freedom of association and meta-communities across platforms.
Mentions community oversight and governance of AI usage.

mappings.title: Ensuring Balance and Preventing Weaponization of Principles
mappings.author: David Bray, PhD

dp_links.summary: Asks how well-intended principles could be weaponized against free societies.
Warns that principles require balance and cannot be treated as absolutes.

mappings.title: Preserving Real History and Strengthening Cybersecurity
mappings.author: Paul Werbos

dp_links.summary: Calls for archives of what has really happened and preservation of real history.
Emphasizes cybersecurity and secure historical records as survival needs.
Links transparency and trustworthy records to resisting fake history.

mappings.title: Designing a Balanced Meta-Layer to Prevent Principle Weaponization
mappings.author: Anon

dp_links.summary: Argues the meta-layer must proactively design rules that balance privacy, safety, agency, and accountability.
Frames balanced transparency and trust as protection against weaponized principles.
Names privacy as a principle that must be balanced rather than made absolute.
Names agency as one of the principles to preserve in system design.

mappings.title: Critique of Decentralization and Bitcoin in Meta-Layer Principles
mappings.author: Dave Troy

mappings.title: Incorporating Security and Privacy in the Meta-Layer
mappings.author: Vandana Upadhyay

dp_links.summary: Discusses authentication and verifiable identities while preserving context-specific anonymity.
Makes privacy and data protection foundational rather than optional.
Argues security must be designed into the meta-layer and IoT ecosystem from the start.
Balances trust, identity, anonymity, and transparency in secure systems.

Vandana explicitly discusses context-dependent multiple identities: 'There may not be one identity but multiple identities depending on context and usage.' She uses the SIM authentication analogy. This directly resonates with DP5's decentralized namespace and personal identifiers — notably absent from the draft.

mappings.title: Customizable Software Agents for Security and Privacy
mappings.author: Cindy Mason

dp_links.summary: Proposes customizable personal software agents to protect data privacy and user preferences.
Frames agents as security guardrails between users, internal systems, apps, and external systems.
Suggests agents may need registration, accountability, or licensing.
Positions agents as filters and containment boundaries for external interactions.

mappings.title: Preserving Open Letters with Bitcoin Ordinals
mappings.author: Anon

dp_links.summary: Uses Bitcoin Ordinals as durable civic memory for preserving open letters and cultural artifacts.
Treats ordinals as immutable provenance and backup for important public records.
Supports transparency and posterity without necessarily promoting Bitcoin as ideology.

mappings.title: Visualizing Customizable Software Agents as Guardrails
mappings.author: Cindy Mason

mappings.title: Security and Privacy as Pillars of the Meta-Layer
mappings.author: Anon

dp_links.summary: Develops decentralized identities, DID protocols, and contextual authentication.
Places privacy and decentralized control at the core of the meta-layer.
Extends security-first design to IoT, devices, authentication, and trusted networks.
Discusses multiple identities and identifiers that vary by context.
